questions.stem: The boy _____ won the first prize in the English speech contest is my deskmate.
questions.explanation: 选A。先行词'the boy'指人，在定语从句中作主语，所以用who。which用于指物；whom用于指人但作宾语；whose表示'……的'，后需接名词。解题口诀：人物主who宾whom，物品which皆可通。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
—I _____ my homework already, so I can go out with you now.
—Great! Let's go to the park.
questions.explanation: 选B。句中有already（已经），这是现在完成时的标志性时间状语，强调过去动作对现在的影响（现在可以出去玩了）。A是一般过去时，单纯陈述过去事实，不与already连用；C是一般现在时；D是现在进行时。解题技巧：看到already/yet/just/since/for等词，优先考虑现在完成时have/has done。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: These used books _____ to the school library every Friday afternoon.
questions.explanation: 选C。主语'books'与动词'return'是被动关系（书被归还），且时间状语'every Friday'表示经常性动作，用一般现在时被动语态am/is/are done。D是一般过去时被动，用于过去特定时间；A是主动语态；B是过去式或过去分词，不能单独作谓语。解题技巧：先看主谓关系（被动用be done），再看时间状语（every day用一般现在时）。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The lights in the classroom are still on. Someone _____ be there preparing for tomorrow's exam.
questions.explanation: 选A。根据前句'灯还亮着'，可做出肯定推测'一定有人在那里'。must表示肯定推测（一定是）；can't表示否定推测（不可能）；might表示不确定推测（可能）；mustn't表示禁止（不许）。解题技巧：推测语气分三级——must一定（肯定），might或许（不确定），can't不可能（否定）。mustn't不是推测，是禁止。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: Please _____ your dictionary to class tomorrow. We will need it for the reading test.
questions.explanation: 选B。bring表示'带来'（从别处带到说话人所在处）；take表示'带走'（从说话人处带到别处）；carry强调'搬运'（负重）；fetch强调'去取来'（往返）。老师让学生明天把字典带到教室来，是朝说话人方向移动，用bring。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: If you don't know the meaning of this word, you can _____ it _____ in the dictionary.
questions.explanation: 选C。look up表示'查阅（词典、资料）'；look after表示'照顾'；look for表示'寻找'；look out表示'小心'。在词典中查单词用look up。这是固定搭配，需要记忆。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: It was very _____ of you to leave the door unlocked when you went out. (care)
questions.explanation: 选B。根据句意'出门不锁门'可知是'粗心的'。careless（形容词）= care + less（否定后缀，表示'无……的'）；careful（细心的）；carefully（副词）；care（名词/动词）。句型It was + adj. + of sb. to do sth.，需要形容词，且根据语境选否定含义。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: 选A。make a box表示'制作一个盒子'，符合语境'我们为纸张制作了一个特殊的回收箱'。buy（买）与下文学生亲手参与制作的语境不符；find（发现）和draw（画）不能与box构成合理搭配。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a green project called "Zero Waste Classroom". The goal was to reduce waste and teach students about environmental protection.

In our class, we (8)_____ a special recycling box for paper. Every day, students would put used paper into it instead of throwing it away. At first, some classmates thought it was (9)_____ and didn't want to participate. However, after our teacher explained how many trees could be saved, they changed their minds.

By the end of the month, we (10)_____ over 50 kilograms of paper. The school also organized a competition between classes to see who could collect the most recyclables. Our class didn't win first place, but we learned a valuable (11)_____ about working together to protect the environment.

Now, the recycling habit has become part of our daily (12)_____. We plan to continue this project next term and hope more students will join us.

questions.explanation: 选C。根据下文'didn't want to participate（不想参与）'可知，起初同学觉得这件事'无聊'。difficult（困难的）虽然也可能导致不想参与，但下文提到老师解释后他们改变了想法，说明之前是认识问题（觉得环保无聊）而非能力问题；important和interesting与'didn't want to participate'逻辑矛盾。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a green project called "Zero Waste Classroom". The goal was to reduce waste and teach students about environmental protection.

In our class, we (8)_____ a special recycling box for paper. Every day, students would put used paper into it instead of throwing it away. At first, some classmates thought it was (9)_____ and didn't want to participate. However, after our teacher explained how many trees could be saved, they changed their minds.

By the end of the month, we (10)_____ over 50 kilograms of paper. The school also organized a competition between classes to see who could collect the most recyclables. Our class didn't win first place, but we learned a valuable (11)_____ about working together to protect the environment.

Now, the recycling habit has become part of our daily (12)_____. We plan to continue this project next term and hope more students will join us.

questions.explanation: 选A。collect表示'收集'，符合回收项目的语境（收集废纸）。produce（生产）、waste（浪费）、burn（燃烧）都与环保回收的主题相悖。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a green project called "Zero Waste Classroom". The goal was to reduce waste and teach students about environmental protection.

In our class, we (8)_____ a special recycling box for paper. Every day, students would put used paper into it instead of throwing it away. At first, some classmates thought it was (9)_____ and didn't want to participate. However, after our teacher explained how many trees could be saved, they changed their minds.

By the end of the month, we (10)_____ over 50 kilograms of paper. The school also organized a competition between classes to see who could collect the most recyclables. Our class didn't win first place, but we learned a valuable (11)_____ about working together to protect the environment.

Now, the recycling habit has become part of our daily (12)_____. We plan to continue this project next term and hope more students will join us.

questions.explanation: 选A。learn a lesson是固定搭配，表示'学到一课/得到教训/获得启示'。learn a skill（学习技能）虽可搭配，但下文'about working together'是抽象的启示而非具体技能；learn a time搭配不当；story（故事）不符合语境。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a green project called "Zero Waste Classroom". The goal was to reduce waste and teach students about environmental protection.

In our class, we (8)_____ a special recycling box for paper. Every day, students would put used paper into it instead of throwing it away. At first, some classmates thought it was (9)_____ and didn't want to participate. However, after our teacher explained how many trees could be saved, they changed their minds.

By the end of the month, we (10)_____ over 50 kilograms of paper. The school also organized a competition between classes to see who could collect the most recyclables. Our class didn't win first place, but we learned a valuable (11)_____ about working together to protect the environment.

Now, the recycling habit has become part of our daily (12)_____. We plan to continue this project next term and hope more students will join us.

questions.explanation: 选C。routine表示'日常惯例/常规'，daily routine是固定搭配，表示'日常生活/日常惯例'。daily study（日常学习）、daily lesson（日常课程）、daily work（日常工作）都不如routine准确，因为回收习惯是融入日常生活的例行公事。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a green project called "Zero Waste Classroom". The goal was to reduce waste and teach students about environmental protection.

In our class, we (8)_____ a special recycling box for paper. Every day, students would put used paper into it instead of throwing it away. At first, some classmates thought it was (9)_____ and didn't want to participate. However, after our teacher explained how many trees could be saved, they changed their minds.

By the end of the month, we (10)_____ over 50 kilograms of paper. The school also organized a competition between classes to see who could collect the most recyclables. Our class didn't win first place, but we learned a valuable (11)_____ about working together to protect the environment.

Now, the recycling habit has become part of our daily (12)_____. We plan to continue this project next term and hope more students will join us.

questions.stem: What problem did Maria notice at school before she invented the backpack?
options: A. Students didn't have enough backpacks.
B. There were not enough charging ports for devices.
C. Phones and tablets were too expensive for students.
D. The classroom had no electricity supply.
questions.explanation: 选B。细节理解题。根据第二段'I saw my classmates fighting over the limited sockets in the classroom'（我看到同学们争抢教室里有限的插座）可知，问题是充电插座不够。A、C、D在文中均未提及或曲解文意。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解
questions.group_passage:
Maria, a 14-year-old student from Shenzhen, never thought her science project would become so popular. Last semester, she invented a solar-powered backpack that can charge electronic devices.

The idea came to her when she noticed that many students in her school struggled to find charging ports for their phones and tablets. "I saw my classmates fighting over the limited sockets in the classroom," Maria explained. "I wondered if there was a better way."

Using flexible solar panels (柔性太阳能板) attached to the backpack's surface, Maria's invention can convert sunlight into electricity. The energy is then stored in a small battery pack inside the bag. On a sunny day, it can fully charge a smartphone in about two hours.

What makes her design special is its practicality. The backpack looks just like a regular school bag, and the solar panels are waterproof (防水的) and lightweight. "I tested it for a month before the competition," said Maria. "Even on cloudy days, it could still collect enough power for emergency use."

Maria's teacher, Mr. Chen, praised her creativity. "She not only solved a real problem but also used green energy," he said. "This is exactly the kind of innovation we want to encourage."

The backpack has won several awards at science fairs. Maria hopes to improve it further by adding more USB ports and making the panels more efficient.

questions.stem: What can we infer about Maria from the passage?
options: A. She doesn't like using modern technology.
B. She only cares about winning science competitions.
C. She is observant of daily life problems and creative.
D. She wants to stop students from using mobile phones.
questions.explanation: 选C。推理判断题。从Maria观察到同学抢插座的问题（observant善于观察），并发明太阳能背包解决问题（creative有创造力），可以推断出C正确。A与事实相反（她发明了科技产品）；B过于片面（她希望改进产品帮助更多人，不只是为了获奖）；D曲解文意（她是解决充电问题，而非阻止使用手机）。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断
questions.group_passage:
Maria, a 14-year-old student from Shenzhen, never thought her science project would become so popular. Last semester, she invented a solar-powered backpack that can charge electronic devices.

The idea came to her when she noticed that many students in her school struggled to find charging ports for their phones and tablets. "I saw my classmates fighting over the limited sockets in the classroom," Maria explained. "I wondered if there was a better way."

Using flexible solar panels (柔性太阳能板) attached to the backpack's surface, Maria's invention can convert sunlight into electricity. The energy is then stored in a small battery pack inside the bag. On a sunny day, it can fully charge a smartphone in about two hours.

What makes her design special is its practicality. The backpack looks just like a regular school bag, and the solar panels are waterproof (防水的) and lightweight. "I tested it for a month before the competition," said Maria. "Even on cloudy days, it could still collect enough power for emergency use."

Maria's teacher, Mr. Chen, praised her creativity. "She not only solved a real problem but also used green energy," he said. "This is exactly the kind of innovation we want to encourage."

The backpack has won several awards at science fairs. Maria hopes to improve it further by adding more USB ports and making the panels more efficient.

questions.stem: The underlined word "convert" in Paragraph 3 probably means "_____".
questions.explanation: 选C。词义猜测题。根据上下文'sunlight into electricity'（阳光变成电），可知convert表示'转换、转变'。太阳能板将阳光'转换'为电能。store（储存）是下一步动作（stored in a battery）；waste（浪费）和consume（消耗）与文意相反。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测
questions.group_passage:

What makes her design special is its practicality. The backpack looks just like a regular school bag, and the solar panels are waterproof (防水的) and lightweight. "I tested it for a month before the competition," said Maria. "Even on cloudy days, it could still collect enough power for emergency use."

Maria's teacher, Mr. Chen, praised her creativity. "She not only solved a real problem but also used green energy," he said. "This is exactly the kind of innovation we want to encourage."

questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: A. How to Make a Solar-Powered Backpack
B. A Student's Green Invention for School Life
C. The History of Solar Power Technology
D. Problems with Mobile Phones in Schools
questions.explanation: 选B。主旨大意题。文章主要讲述学生Maria发明太阳能背包解决校园充电难题的故事，突出学生的创造力和环保能源的使用。A过于侧重制作过程（文中未详细描述制作步骤）；C范围过大（不是讲太阳能历史）；D偏离主题（不是讨论手机问题）。B准确概括了'学生发明'和'绿色能源'两个核心要素。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意
questions.group_passage:
Maria, a 14-year-old student from Shenzhen, never thought her science project would become so popular. Last semester, she invented a solar-powered backpack that can charge electronic devices.

The idea came to her when she noticed that many students in her school struggled to find charging ports for their phones and tablets. "I saw my classmates fighting over the limited sockets in the classroom," Maria explained. "I wondered if there was a better way."

Using flexible solar panels (柔性太阳能板) attached to the backpack's surface, Maria's invention can convert sunlight into electricity. The energy is then stored in a small battery pack inside the bag. On a sunny day, it can fully charge a smartphone in about two hours.

What makes her design special is its practicality. The backpack looks just like a regular school bag, and the solar panels are waterproof (防水的) and lightweight. "I tested it for a month before the competition," said Maria. "Even on cloudy days, it could still collect enough power for emergency use."

Maria's teacher, Mr. Chen, praised her creativity. "She not only solved a real problem but also used green energy," he said. "This is exactly the kind of innovation we want to encourage."

The backpack has won several awards at science fairs. Maria hopes to improve it further by adding more USB ports and making the panels more efficient.

options: A. This means studying at the same time every day.
B. However, with the right methods, anyone can improve.
C. Therefore, take a short break every 45 minutes.
D. Discussing problems with others can deepen your understanding.
E. In addition, make sure you get enough sleep every night.
questions.explanation: 选B。前文说'很多学生发现平衡学习和休息很难'，后文说'这里有一些建议'，中间需要转折过渡，表示'虽然有困难，但有方法可以改善'。However表示转折，符合逻辑。其他选项：A解释schedule，C讲休息，D讲讨论，E讲睡眠，均与上下文衔接不当。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students find it hard to balance study and rest. (17)_____ Here are some tips that might help you become a better learner.

First, create a study schedule and stick to it. (18)_____ For example, if you focus best in the morning, arrange your difficult subjects then.

Second, don't study for too long without breaks. Research shows that our brains need rest to process information. (19)_____ You could take a 10-minute walk or simply look out the window.

Finally, find a study partner or join a study group. (20)_____ When you explain concepts to others, you understand them better yourself.

Remember, good study habits take time to develop. Start with small changes and be patient with yourself.

options: A. This means studying at the same time every day.
B. However, with the right methods, anyone can improve.
C. Therefore, take a short break every 45 minutes.
D. Discussing problems with others can deepen your understanding.
E. In addition, make sure you get enough sleep every night.
questions.explanation: 选A。前文提到'create a study schedule（制定学习时间表）'，A选项'这意味着每天在相同的时间学习'是对schedule的解释说明，且与后文'例如，如果你早上注意力最好……'衔接自然。其他选项与'schedule'主题无关。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students find it hard to balance study and rest. (17)_____ Here are some tips that might help you become a better learner.

First, create a study schedule and stick to it. (18)_____ For example, if you focus best in the morning, arrange your difficult subjects then.

Second, don't study for too long without breaks. Research shows that our brains need rest to process information. (19)_____ You could take a 10-minute walk or simply look out the window.

Finally, find a study partner or join a study group. (20)_____ When you explain concepts to others, you understand them better yourself.

Remember, good study habits take time to develop. Start with small changes and be patient with yourself.

options: A. This means studying at the same time every day.
B. However, with the right methods, anyone can improve.
C. Therefore, take a short break every 45 minutes.
D. Discussing problems with others can deepen your understanding.
E. In addition, make sure you get enough sleep every night.
questions.explanation: 选C。前文讲'大脑需要休息来处理信息'，C选项'Therefore（因此）'表示因果关系，建议'每45分钟休息一次'，且与后文'你可以散步10分钟……'衔接紧密。Therefore表示'因此、所以'，用于引出结论或建议。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students find it hard to balance study and rest. (17)_____ Here are some tips that might help you become a better learner.

First, create a study schedule and stick to it. (18)_____ For example, if you focus best in the morning, arrange your difficult subjects then.

Second, don't study for too long without breaks. Research shows that our brains need rest to process information. (19)_____ You could take a 10-minute walk or simply look out the window.

Finally, find a study partner or join a study group. (20)_____ When you explain concepts to others, you understand them better yourself.

Remember, good study habits take time to develop. Start with small changes and be patient with yourself.

options: A. This means studying at the same time every day.
B. However, with the right methods, anyone can improve.
C. Therefore, take a short break every 45 minutes.
D. Discussing problems with others can deepen your understanding.
E. In addition, make sure you get enough sleep every night.
questions.explanation: 选D。本段主题是'find a study partner（找学习伙伴）'，D选项'与他人讨论问题可以加深理解'解释了为什么要找学习伙伴，且与后文'当你向别人解释概念时，你自己理解得更好'形成递进关系。注意E选项虽然也是建议，但属于额外补充，不如D贴合'partner'主题。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students find it hard to balance study and rest. (17)_____ Here are some tips that might help you become a better learner.

First, create a study schedule and stick to it. (18)_____ For example, if you focus best in the morning, arrange your difficult subjects then.

Second, don't study for too long without breaks. Research shows that our brains need rest to process information. (19)_____ You could take a 10-minute walk or simply look out the window.

Finally, find a study partner or join a study group. (20)_____ When you explain concepts to others, you understand them better yourself.

Remember, good study habits take time to develop. Start with small changes and be patient with yourself.
